title: Menu Items
text: Menu Items A menu item can be a menu option that results in an action, a reference to another menu, such as a cascaded menu or included menu, or a separator for grouping items. Menu items have associated triggers in which to program the conditions under which they are displayed and the action they perform. You can define the following types of menu item: Table 1. Types of Menu Items Item type Available In Triggers Description Menu Option Drop-down Pop-up preDisplay select Performs an action, such as activating a form or structure editor function. Dynamic Menu Drop-down Pop-up preDisplay select Placeholder where additional menu items can be inserted at runtime by $inlinemenu ProcScript statements in the preDisplay trigger. Cascaded Menu Drop-down Pop-up preDisplay References a drop-down menu, displaying its contents as a separate menu cascading from the current menu. Use a cascading menu to include additional items in a menu that would otherwise cause the menu to be too long. For usability, don't define more than three levels of cascading menus. Included Menu Drop-down Pop-up None References a drop-down menu, copying its contents into the current menu during compilation. Use included menus to avoid duplicating menu item definitions. Separator Drop-down Pop-up preDisplay Horizontal dividing line used to separate groups of related items. Drop-down Menu Menu Bar preDisplay References a drop-down menu. Right Align Marker Menu Bar preDisplay Causes subsequent items to be displayed on the right side of the application window. Only one of these should or needs to be present in a menu bar. Icons in Menus The text of menu items in cascading and dynamic menus can be preceded by an icon. To add an icon to a menu item, specify the item as a gold-separated string consisting of an image or glyph name and the text. For more information, see Define and Use Menus . Related Links Menu Item Triggers Menu items have associated triggers in which to program the conditions under which they are displayed and the action they perform. ProcScript for Menus You control how a menu item is displayed by using the ProcScript instructions in the preDisplay trigger. Dynamic Menu Items Dynamic menus contain one or more menu items that are generated at runtime, and inserted in a predetermined location of a drop-down or popup menu. They can be used, for example, to display and open recently accessed objects, list and switch between currently open windows, or provide a pop-up menu that is related to the value of a field.
